                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa tiếng việt Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 adopt chấp nhận, áp dụng v /əˈdɒpt/ B2 embrace reject 2 bird-watching ngắm chim n /ˈbɜːd ˌwɒtʃɪŋ/ B2 3 breeding sự sinh sản, gây giống n /ˈbriː.dɪŋ/ B2 reproduction sterilization 4 clean-up hoạt động dọn dẹp n /ˈkliːn ʌp/ B1 5 community cộng đồng n /kəˈmjuːnəti/ A2 society, group individual 6 conservation sự bảo tồn, sự giữ gìn n /ˌkɒn.səˈveɪ.ʃən/ B2 preservation, protection destruction 7 consumption sự tiêu thụ, tiêu dùng n /kənˈsʌmp.ʃən/ B2 saving, accumulation 8 coral reef rạn san hô n /ˈkɒrəl riːf/ B2 9 eco-friendly thân thiện với môi trường adj /ˈiːkəʊ ˈfrendli/ B2 environmentally friendly, sustainable 10 eco-lodge nhà nghỉ sinh thái n /ˈiːkəʊ lɒdʒ/ B2 11 eco-tour chuyến du lịch sinh thái n.phr /ˈiːkəʊ tʊə(r)/ B2 mass tourism 12 ecotourism du lịch sinh thái n /ˈiːkəʊtʊərɪzəm/ /ˈiːkəʊtɔːrɪzəm/ B2 mass tourism 13 educational mang tính giáo dục adj /ˌedʒuˈkeɪʃənl/ B1 noneducational 14 encourage khuyến khích, cổ vũ v /ɪnˈkʌr.ɪdʒ/ B1 motivate discourage 15 existence sự tồn tại, sự sống n /ɪɡˈzɪs.təns/ B2 presence nonexistence 16 fur lông thú n /fɜː(r)/ B1 [Page 2] 17 generate tạo ra, sản xuất ra v /ˈdʒen.ər.eɪt/ B2 produce, create destroy 18 heavily nhiều, mạnh adv /ˈhevɪli/ B1 strongly, greatly lightly, slightly 19 hospitality lòng hiếu khách / ngành dịch vụ n /ˌhɒspɪˈtæləti/ C1 friendliness unfriendliness 20 impact tác động n /ˈɪmpækt/ B1 effect, influence 21...
                                                                                                        1. Question 1. The word “applying” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by _______. A. recovering B. adapting C. breeding D. adopting
                                                                                                        2. Question 2. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the green activities? A. join beach clean-ups B. go on eco-tours C. learn about local wildlife D. visit organic farms
                                                                                                        3. Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Bali only focuses on beaches and culture in tourism. B. The local government in Bali banned single-use plastic in 2022. C. Tourists in Bali can enjoy the island while still protecting the environment. D. Green tourism in Bali has reduced the number of tourists visiting the island.
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#2 | q=4 | nums=4, 5, 6, 7 | p=5-5 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. The giant panda is one of the most famous and loved animals in the world. It lives mainly in the mountains of Sichuan, China. Known for its black and white fur, the panda has become a symbol of wildlife protection. Although the panda’s population has improved, it is still considered a vulnerable species. The main threat to pandas is habitat loss. As forests are cut down for farming and development, pandas lose their homes and food sources. They rely heavily on bamboo, which only grows in certain areas. Climate change also affects bamboo growth, making survival even harder for the species. To protect pandas, the Chinese government has created large nature reserves. Breeding programs and education efforts have also helped raise awareness. Tourists can visit panda centres to learn more and support conservation. Giant pandas are more than just a cute animal. They represent the need to protect other endangered species and their environments. Saving the panda means saving the forests, which benefits many other animals as well. [Page 5]
                                                                                                        1. Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “Although the panda’s population has improved, it is still considered a vulnerable species.” A. The number of pandas is growing fast and they are no longer in danger. B. Even though there are more pandas now, they still face the risk of extinction. C. Pandas are compl...
                                                                                                        2. Question 5. The word “survival” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. A. development B. living C. existence D. death
                                                                                                        3. Question 6. The word “their” in paragraph 4 refers to _______. A. giant pandas B. environments C. the forests D. other endangered species
                                                                                                        4. Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Giant pandas can survive in any climate as long as bamboo is available. B. The panda population is growing so fast that it is no longer a concern. C. The loss of forest land makes it harder for pandas to find food and shelter. D. Tourists are banned from visiting panda centres to protect...
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#3 | q=8 | nums=8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | p=6-6 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. Ecotourism is a form of travel that focuses on exploring natural areas while protecting the environment and supporting local communities. Unlike mass tourism, which often leads to pollution and overdevelopment, ecotourism encourages responsible behaviour. In recent years, it has become a growing trend as more travellers seek meaningful experiences and care about nature. People are now more aware of climate change and want to reduce their impact while travelling. Ecotourism offers many different activities. These include hiking through forests, bird-watching, camping in national parks, snorkelling in coral reefs, and visiting wildlife sanctuaries. It also promotes staying in eco-lodges that use renewable energy and local materials. Tourists often take part in environmental education, tree planting, or cultural exchanges with local people. All these activities are designed to be low-impact and educational. A great example of successful ecotourism is Costa Rica. The country protects over 25% of its land through national parks and reserves. Visitors can enjoy rainforests, waterfalls, and volcanoes while learning about biodiversity. Local people benefit from jobs in guiding, hospitality, and conservation projects. However, ecotourism still has some negative sides. If not managed carefully, too many tourists can harm wildlife or damage ecosystems. Some companies may use the term ‘eco’ just for profit. That’s why true ecotourism needs planning, honesty, and long-term thinking.
                                                                                                        1. Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “People are now more aware of climate change and want to reduce their impact while travelling.” A. Travellers today are less interested in natural beauty and more focused on comfort. B. More and more tourists now avoid travel completely because of its effect on the cl...
                                                                                                        2. Question 9. The word “It” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. A. Wildlife B. Snorkelling C. Ecotourism D. Bird-watching [Page 6]
                                                                                                        3. Question 10. The word “conservation” in paragraph 3 is CLOSEST in meaning to_______. A. threat B. protection C. consumption D. creation
                                                                                                        4. Question 11. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the places visitors can enjoy in Costa Rica? A. eco-lodges B. rainforests C. waterfalls D. volcanoes
                                                                                                        5. Question 12. The word “damage” in paragraph 4 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. A. destroy B. generate C. release D. repair
                                                                                                        6. Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Ecotourism needs proper management to avoid harming the environment. B. Tourists in Costa Rica are not allowed to enter national parks and reserves. C. All companies that promote ecotourism always follow eco-friendly practices. D. Cultural exchanges are not considered part of ecotourism...
                                                                                                        7. Question 14. In which paragraph does the author explore the drawbacks of ecotourism? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4
                                                                                                        8. Question 15. In which paragraph does the author compare ecotourism to another kind of tourism? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4

                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa tiếng việt Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 adjust điều chỉnh v /əˈdʒʌst/ B2 modify 2 battery life thời lượng pin n. phr /ˈbæt.ər.i laɪf/ B2 3 combine kết hợp v /kəmˈbaɪn/ B2 mix separate, divide 4 communication giao tiếp, truyền thông n /kəˌmjuː.nɪˈkeɪ.ʃən/ B1 5 compact nhỏ gọn adj /ˈkɒm.pækt/ B2 small large, bulky 6 convenience sự tiện lợi n /kənˈviː.ni.əns/ B2 inconvenience 7 cordless không dây adj /ˈkɔːd.ləs/ B2 wired 8 design thiết kế n/v /dɪˈzaɪn/ A1 9 discovery sự khám phá, khám phá n /dɪˈskʌv.ər.i/ B2 finding 10 driverless không người lái adj /ˈdraɪ.və.ləs/ B2 autonomous, self-driving 11 efficient hiệu quả adj /ɪˈfɪʃ.ənt/ B1 effective inefficient 12 electronic device thiết bị điện tử n. phr /ɪˌlekˈtrɒn.ɪk dɪˈvaɪs/ B2 13 explore khám phá v /ɪkˈsplɔː.r/ B2 discover 14 feature có đặc điểm, nổi bật với, đặc điểm v/n /ˈfiː.tʃər/ B2 characteristic, attribute 15 function chức năng, hoạt động n/v /ˈfʌŋk.ʃən/ B2 16 gadget thiết bị, dụng cụ nhỏ n /ˈɡædʒ.ɪt/ C1 device, tool 17 invent phát minh v /ɪnˈvent/ B1 create 18 invention phát minh, sáng chế n /ɪnˈven.ʃən/ B1 creation [Page 2] 19 inventor nhà phát minh n /ɪnˈven.tər/ B2 20 launch ra mắt v /lɔːntʃ/ B2 release recall 21 performance hiệu suất n /pəˈfɔː.məns/ B2 22 portable di động, dễ mang theo adj /ˈpɔː.tə.bəl/ C1 23 productivity năng suất n /ˌprɒd.ʌkˈtɪv.ɪ.ti/ B2 24 rechargeable có thể sạc lại adj /ˌriːˈtʃ...

He also experimented with wireless (21) _______ and radio waves far ahead of his time. Brilliant yet humble, Tesla’s imagination and dedication continue to inspire scientists, engineers, and dreamers everywhere.

                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa tiếng việt Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 accessible có thể tiếp cận được adj /əkˈses.ə.bəl/ C1 inaccessible 2 adoption sự áp dụng n /əˈdɒp.ʃən/ C1 3 anatomy giải phẫu n /əˈnæt.ə.mi/ C2 4 arrival sự đến / xuất hiện n /əˈraɪ.vəl/ B1/B2 advent, appearance 5 artificial intelligence trí tuệ nhân tạo n.p /ˌɑː.tɪˈfɪʃ.əl ɪnˈtel.ɪ.dʒəns/ B2 6 audience khán giả n /ˈɔː.di.əns/ A2 7 audio-visual nghe nhìn adj /ˌɔː.di.oʊˈvɪʒ.u.əl/ B2 8 computer-generated được tạo bởi máy tính adj /kəmˌpjuː.tər ˈdʒen.ə.reɪ.tɪd/ C1 9 concept khái niệm n /ˈkɒn.sept/ B2 idea, conception, notion 10 connect kết nối v /kəˈnekt/ A2 disconnect 11 consistently một cách nhất quán adv /kənˈsɪs.tənt.li/ B2 inconsistently 12 cybersecurity an ninh mạng n /ˌsaɪ.bə.sɪˈkjʊə.rə.ti/ C1 13 discourse diễn ngôn n /ˈdɪs.kɔːs/ C1 14 dominate chiếm ưu thế v /ˈdɒm.ɪ.neɪt/ B2 15 error lỗi, sai sót n /ˈer.ər/ A2 mistake accuracy, correctness 16 evolve tiến hóa, phát triển v /ɪˈvɒlv/ B2 develop [Page 2] 17 field trip chuyến tham quan (thường là mục đích học tập) n.p /ˈfiːld ˌtrɪp/ B1 18 headset tai nghe có mic / bộ tai nghe n /ˈhed.set/ B1 19 historical site di tích lịch sử n.p /hɪˈstɒr.ɪ.kəl saɪt/ B1 20 immersive chìm đắm, sống động (cảm giác nhập vai) adj /ɪˈmɜː.sɪv/ C1 21 instant ngay lập tức adj /ˈɪn.stənt/ B2 immediate 22 interact tương tác v /ˌɪn.təˈrækt/ B2 23 interactive có tính tương tác adj /ˌ...
                                                                                                        1. Question 1. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the advantages of driverless vehicles? A. reduce accidents B. choose the best routes C. save fuel and money D. follow traffic regulations
                                                                                                        2. Question 2. The word “expensive” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. A. outdated B. modern C. costly D. affordable
                                                                                                        3. Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Weather conditions have no effect on the performance of driverless cars. B. Driverless cars cost less than traditional vehicles. C. Advanced sensors help improve the safety of driverless cars. D. Professional drivers will not be affected by the rise of driverless vehicles.
                                                                                                        reading_group seg#2 | q=4 | nums=4, 5, 6, 7 | p=5-5 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. Virtual Reality (VR) is a computer-generated simulation that allows users to experience and interact with a three-dimensional environment as if they were physically present. By wearing a VR headset, users can look around, move, and engage with virtual objects in real time. This technology creates an immersive and realistic sense of presence. In education, VR offers students a chance to explore concepts beyond the limits of the classroom. For example, they can take virtual field trips to historical sites, dive into the human body to study anatomy, or conduct science experiments in a safe virtual lab. Such experiences make learning more engaging and help students understand complex ideas through direct interaction. VR can improve retention, enhance motivation, and bridge the gap between theory and practice. It also makes education more accessible, allowing learners from different locations to share the same virtual space. As VR technology becomes more affordable and advanced, its role in education is expected to grow.
                                                                                                        1. Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? “In education, VR offers students a chance to explore concepts beyond the limits of the classroom.” A. VR allows learners to study ideas and topics outside classroom boundaries. B. With VR, students’ understanding is confined to the classroom environment. C. VR replac...
                                                                                                        2. Question 5. The word “allows” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by _______. A. encourages B. hinders C. enables D. hesitates
                                                                                                        3. Question 6. The word “It” in paragraph 3 refers to _______. A. VR B. retention C. motivation D. theory
                                                                                                        4. Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Virtual field trips replace all traditional classroom lessons. B. Wearing a VR headset lets users interact with objects in real time. C. VR only helps with science subjects, not history or anatomy. D. The cost of VR technology is currently too high for schools.
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#3 | q=8 | nums=8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | p=6-6 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. The earliest stage of mass communication was dominated by print media. Newspapers, magazines, and books allowed information to be recorded, stored, and shared widely. The printing press revolutionised literacy and public awareness, enabling ideas to spread across distances faster than word of mouth. This period laid the foundation for modern journalism and public discourse. The invention of radio and later television transformed communication into a real-time, audio-visual experience. Radio brought news, music, and entertainment into homes instantly, while the other added moving images that engaged audiences more deeply. These media created shared national moments, from presidential speeches to major sporting events, and connected people emotionally. The arrival of the internet marked a turning point, allowing instant global communication. Emails replaced letters, websites offered unlimited information, and online news became available 24/7. Social media began to emerge, giving individuals the power to share their own voices alongside traditional media outlets. Today, interactive platforms dominate. People can stream live events, join online discussions, and access personalised content anytime. Media is no longer just consumed—it is created and shaped by users. This stage continues to evolve, pushing communication toward greater speed, personalisation, and global interconnection.
                                                                                                        1. Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “The earliest stage of mass communication was dominated by print media.” A. Print media was not widely used during the earliest stage of mass communication. B. Print media was the primary means of mass communication in its earliest phase. C. The latter mass communicat...
                                                                                                        2. Question 9. The phrase “the other” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. A. radio B. television C. communication D. experience
                                                                                                        3. Question 10. The word “arrival” in paragraph 3 is CLOSEST in meaning to_______. A. risk B. destination C. advent D. disappearance
                                                                                                        4. Question 11. The word “unlimited” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. A. vital B. infinite C. large D. restricted
                                                                                                        5. Question 12. According to paragraph 4, today, thanks to interactive platforms, people can do all of the following, EXCEPT _______. A. joining discussions online B. accessing personalised content C. speeding up communication D. streaming live events [Page 6]
                                                                                                        6. Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. People only consume media and do not create content themselves today. B. Television was the first technology to bring information instantly to homes. C. Social media existed before the invention of the Internet. D. Newspapers helped spread information faster than spoken communication.
                                                                                                        7. Question 14. In which paragraph does the author explore experience relating to hearing and watching? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4
                                                                                                        8. Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention the creation of media by users? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4

(11) _______ by artificial intelligence, it can map dangerous environments, detect heat (12) _______ from survivors, and transmit data through wireless networks.                                                                                                         [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa tiếng việt Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 AI-powered được vận hành bởi AI adj /ˌeɪ.aɪ ˈpaʊ.əd/ B2 AI-driven manual 2 diagnosis sự chẩn đoán n /ˌdaɪ.əɡˈnəʊ.sɪs/ C1 3 recognition sự nhận diện n /ˌrek.əɡˈnɪʃ.ən/ B2 identification 4 recovery sự hồi phục n /rɪˈkʌv.ər.i/ B2 5 appointment cuộc hẹn n /əˈpɔɪnt.mənt/ B1 6 predictive có tính dự đoán adj /prɪˈdɪk.tɪv/ B2 7 human-centred lấy con người làm trung tâm adj /ˌhjuː.mən ˈsen.təd/ B2 people-oriented 8 manual thủ công adj /ˈmæn.ju.əl/ C1 hand-operated automatic 9 labour lao động n /ˈleɪ.bər/ B2 10 data analysis phân tích dữ liệu n. phr /ˈdeɪ.tə əˈnæl.ə.sɪs/ B2 11 digital kỹ thuật số adj /ˈdɪdʒ.ɪ.təl/ A2 12 platform n /ˈplæt.fɔːm/ B2 13 irrigation sự tưới tiêu n /ˌɪr.ɪˈɡeɪ.ʃən/ C1 watering 14 accurately chính xác adv /ˈæk.jʊ.rət.li/ B2 precisely inaccurately 15 combine kết hợp v /kəmˈbaɪn/ B1 merge separate 16 humidity độ ẩm n /hjuːˈmɪd.ə.ti/ B2 dampness dryness 17 crop yield năng suất mùa màng n. phr /ˌkrɒp ˈjiːld/ B2 18 production sự sản xuất n /prəˈdʌk.ʃən/ B1 manufacturing 19 food shortage sự thiếu hụt lương thực n. phr /ˌfuːd ˈʃɔː.tɪdʒ/ B2 [Page 2] 20 agricultural thuộc về nông nghiệp adj /ˌæɡ.rɪˈkʌl.tʃər.əl/ C1 farming 21 simulate mô phỏng v /ˈsɪm.jʊ.leɪt/ C1 imitate real 22 integrate tích hợp v /ˈɪn.tɪ.ɡreɪt/ B2 incorporate separate 23 accessible có thể tiếp cận adj /əkˈses.ə.bəl/ C1 reachable inaccessible 24 ef...
                                                                                                        1. Question 1. The word “accurate” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. A. automated B. remarkable C. wrong D. precise
                                                                                                        2. Question 2. According to paragraph 2, AI helps hospitals manage work by all of the following, EXCEPT _______. A. scheduling appointments B. answering patients’ questions C. cutting costs D. planning treatments
                                                                                                        3. Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. AI completely replaces doctors in diagnosing diseases. B. AI can only be used in hospitals for scheduling appointments. C. Image recognition programs can detect cancer signs faster than doctors. D. Privacy and cost are no longer concerns in applying AI to healthcare.
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#2 | q=4 | nums=4, 5, 6, 7 | p=4-4 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. Modern agriculture is no longer only about manual labour and traditional methods. Farmers today rely on advanced machines, data analysis, and digital platforms to increase productivity. Smart systems monitor soil quality, control irrigation, and predict weather patterns more accurately than humans. By combining technology with farming, agriculture becomes more sustainable and efficient. One of the most useful innovations is precision farming. Sensors placed in the field measure temperature, humidity, and nutrient levels, while drones capture images of crops from above. These tools help farmers decide when to water, fertilise, or harvest. Artificial intelligence supports this process by turning raw data into clear suggestions, saving time and resources while improving crop yield. The benefits go beyond higher production. Smarter farming methods also reduce waste, use less water, and limit harmful chemicals, which protects the environment. In developing countries, such technology can help communities fight food shortages and adapt to climate change. Although AI is important, the main goal remains to build a stronger and more sustainable agricultural system for the future. [Page 4]
                                                                                                        1. Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “By combining technology with farming, agriculture becomes more sustainable and efficient.” A. Mixing farming with technology increases sustainability but lowers productivity. B. The use of technology in farming helps improve efficiency but reduces sustainability. C. ...
                                                                                                        2. Question 5. The phrase “These tools” in paragraph 2 refers to _______. A. useful innovations B. images of crops C. nutrient levels D. sensors and drones
                                                                                                        3. Question 6. The word “shortages” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. A. abundances B. limitations C. abilities D. products
                                                                                                        4. Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Modern agriculture depends largely on manual labour and traditional methods. B. Precision farming uses sensors and drones to help improve crop yield. C. Farmers use drones only to water their crops. D. The main goal of AI in farming is to replace farmers and make it sustainable.
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#3 | q=8 | nums=8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | p=5-5 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. An AI chatbot is a computer program designed to simulate human conversation through text or voice. Using natural language processing and machine learning, it can understand questions, provide answers, and even maintain long discussions. Chatbots are now integrated into websites, messaging apps, and digital platforms, making communication between humans and machines more natural and accessible. The most obvious advantage of this technology is efficiency. In customer service, a chatbot can respond instantly to thousands of users at the same time, solving basic problems without human staff. In education, it supports students with explanations and practice tasks. Even in daily life, people rely on chatbots for translation, scheduling, or quick information. This makes services faster, cheaper, and more convenient. However, chatbots are far from perfect. They sometimes misunderstand context, provide inaccurate information, or sound unnatural in sensitive conversations. Unlike humans, they cannot show genuine empathy or creativity. Overreliance on pre-trained data also limits their ability to deal with unexpected questions. These weaknesses remind us that chatbots are tools, not replacements for human intelligence. A more serious problem is overuse or misuse. Some people depend too heavily on chatbots for homework, writing, or decision-making, which may reduce critical thinking skills. Others worry about “brainwashing,” since users can be influenced by biased or manipulated responses. Therefore, while chatbots bring many benefits, society must use them responsibly to avoid dependency and maintain human judgment.
                                                                                                        1. Question 8. The word “it” in paragraph 1 refers to ______. A. machine learning B. an AI chatbot C. text or voice D. human conversation
                                                                                                        2. Question 9. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as one of the abilities of AI chatbots in paragraph 1? A. learn foreign languages B. keep long discussions C. give answers D. understand questions [Page 5]
                                                                                                        3. Question 10. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? “Overreliance on pre-trained data also limits their ability to deal with unexpected questions.” A. Relying too much on pre-trained data makes them less effective with unfamiliar questions. B. Heavy use of pre-trained data makes chatbots better at managing predictable...
                                                                                                        4. Question 11. The word “biased” in paragraph 4 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. A. fair B. wrong C. right D. unequal
                                                                                                        5. Question 12. According to the passage, what role do chatbots play in education? A. They fully replace teachers by designing entire curricula. B. They mainly provide explanations and practice tasks for students. C. They organise school schedules and manage teachers’ workloads. D. They decide which subjects students should focus on.
                                                                                                        6. Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Customer service no longer requires human employees as problems are solved by chatbots. B. In education, students always receive perfect answers and full creativity from chatbots. C. Services become faster and more convenient when chatbots handle basic tasks. D. Sensitive conversations ...
                                                                                                        7. Question 14. In which paragraph does the author mention problems with thinking ability? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4
                                                                                                        8. Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention some fields AI chatbots can help? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4

                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 vertical farm nông trại thẳng đứng n.phr /ˈvɜːtɪkl fɑːm/ C1 2 building tòa nhà n /ˈbɪldɪŋ/ A2 3 city resident cư dân thành phố n.phr /ˈsɪti ˈrezɪdənt/ B1 urban dweller 4 urban sprawl bành trướng đô thị n.phr /ˌɜːbən ˈsprɔːl/ C1 5 green space không gian xanh n.phr /ɡriːn speɪs/ B2 6 air quality chất lượng không khí n.phr /eə(r) ˈkwɒləti/ B1 7 living wage mức lương đủ sống n.phr /ˈlɪvɪŋ weɪdʒ/ C1 8 condition điều kiện, tình trạng n /kənˈdɪʃn/ B1 state 9 inequality sự bất bình đẳng n /ˌɪnɪˈkwɒləti/ C2 equality 10 densely populated dân cư đông đúc adj /ˈdensli ˈpɒpjuleɪtɪd/ B2 11 overcrowding tình trạng quá đông, tình trạng quá tải dân số n /ˌəʊvəˈkraʊdɪŋ/ C1 12 shortage sự thiếu hụt n /ˈʃɔːtɪdʒ/ B2 lack, scarcity abundance 13 waste management quản lý rác thải n.phr /weɪst ˈmænɪdʒmənt/ C1 14 digital road đường kỹ thuật số n.phr /ˈdɪdʒɪtl rəʊd/ C1 15 moving walkway n.phr /ˈmuːvɪŋ ˈwɔːkweɪ/ C1 băng chuyền di chuyển, thang băng chuyền, đường đi bộ di động 16 motorway đường cao tốc n /ˈməʊtəweɪ/ A2 [Page 2] 17 convenient tiện lợi adj /kənˈviːniənt/ B1 inconvenient 18 migrate di cư v /maɪˈɡreɪt/ B2 19 urban thuộc đô thị adj /ˈɜːbən/ B2 20 locate định vị, đặt ở v /ləʊˈkeɪt/ B1 21 living standard mức sống n.phr /ˈlɪvɪŋ ˈstændəd/ B2 22 living cost chi phí sinh hoạt n.phr /ˈlɪvɪŋ kɒst/ B1 cost of living 23 growth sự tăng trưởng, sự phát ...

                                                                                                        3. Immigrants in big cities often face many difficulties.                                                                                                         1. Question 7. A. growth B. space C. wage D. slum
                                                                                                        2. Question 8. A. walkways B. residents C. motorways D. conditions

                                                                                                        4. The most densely (10) _______ city in the world is Dhaka, the capital of Bangladesh. Housing (12) _______ force many families to live in slums with poor conditions. In addition, waste (13) _______ and clean water supply cannot keep up with population growth. These issues make urban planning in Dhaka an urgent challenge.
                                                                                                        1. Question 10. A. sustainable B. convenient C. urban D. populated

                                                                                                        6. Question 15. A. spaces B. conditions C. walkways D. shortages

                                                                                                        6. Urbanisation is the process by which more people (18) _______ from rural areas to cities, making cities grow larger. It usually happens because people want better jobs, education, and healthcare that are easier to find in (19) _______ areas. Industrialisation and economic development also push urbanisation, since factories and businesses are mostly (20) _______ in cities. While it can create opportunities and improve living (21) _______, urbanisation also puts pressure on housing, traffic, and the environment.
                                                                                                        1. Question 18. A. migrate B. locate C. manage D. practice
                                                                                                        2. Question 19. A. digital B. urban C. sustainable D. convenient
                                                                                                        3. Question 20. A. practiced B. migrated C. managed D. located
                                                                                                        4. Question 21. A. buildings B. walkways C. management D. standards
                                                                                                        cloze_group seg#6 | q=4 | nums=22, 23, 24, 25 | p=None-None | rule_high
                                                                                                        7. One major problem of urbanisation is overcrowding, which leads to a shortage of housing and higher living (22) _______. Traffic jams and air pollution are common in big cities, affecting people’s health. Moreover, rapid urban (23) _______ often destroys green spaces and increases waste. In some cases, poor planning results in (24) _______, where people live in unsafe conditions. These disadvantages show that urbanisation must be carefully (25) _______ to ensure sustainable development.
                                                                                                        1. Question 22. A. roads B. spaces C. slums D. costs
                                                                                                        2. Question 23. A. building B. growth C. farm D. practice
                                                                                                        3. Question 24. A. lanes B. costs C. slums D. spaces
                                                                                                        4. Question 25. A. managed B. migrated C. practiced D. located
                                                                                                        cloze_group seg#7 | q=5 | nums=26, 27, 28, 29, 30 | p=None-None | rule_high
                                                                                                        8. Copenhagen, the capital of Denmark, is well-known for adopting green (26) _______. The city encourages people to use bicycles instead of cars, and it has built hundreds of kilometres of safe cycle (27) _______. Public transportation in Copenhagen runs mainly on (28) _______ energy, reducing air pollution and noise. In addition, many buildings use eco-friendly designs such as (29) _______ gardens and solar panels. The city also invests in wind energy, which supplies a large part of its electricity. Thanks to these efforts, Copenhagen has become one of the most (30) _______ cities in the world and a model for urban environmental protection.
                                                                                                        1. Question 26. A. spaces B. practices C. slums D. buildings
                                                                                                        2. Question 27. A. lanes B. costs C. shortages D. spaces
                                                                                                        3. Question 28. A. convenient B. urban C. digital D. renewable
                                                                                                        4. Question 29. A. resident B. quality C. living D. rooftop
                                                                                                        5. Question 30. A. urban B. populated C. sustainable D. vertical

                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 coastal thuộc ven biển adj /ˈkəʊstl/ C1 2 major chính, lớn adj /ˈmeɪdʒə(r)/ B1 main, significant minor 3 multicultural đa văn hóa adj /ˌmʌltiˈkʌltʃərəl/ C1 4 architecture kiến trúc n /ˈɑːkɪtektʃə(r)/ B1 5 economy nền kinh tế n /ɪˈkɒnəmi/ B1 6 vibrant sôi động, đầy sức sống adj /ˈvaɪbrənt/ B2 exciting, lively, dynamic 7 welcoming niềm nở, hiếu khách adj /ˈwelkəmɪŋ/ B1 unwelcoming 8 population dân số n /ˌpɒpjuˈleɪʃn/ B1 9 sustainable bền vững adj /səˈsteɪnəbl/ C1 10 possibility khả năng, triển vọng n /ˌpɒsəˈbɪləti/ B1 chance, prospect impossibility 11 rhythm nhịp điệu n /ˈrɪðəm/ B2 12 conversation cuộc trò chuyện n /ˌkɒnvəˈseɪʃn/ A2 discussion 13 charm sức hấp dẫn, quyến rũ n /tʃɑːm/ C1 attraction 14 exist tồn tại v /ɪɡˈzɪst/ B1 survive disappear 15 concrete bê tông n /ˈkɒŋkriːt/ B2 cement 16 diversity sự đa dạng n /daɪˈvɜːsəti/ B2 variety uniformity 17 thrive phát triển mạnh mẽ v /θraɪv/ C1 flourish, prosper 18 transform biến đổi, thay đổi v /trænsˈfɔːm/ B2 convert [Page 2] 19 living standard mức sống phr /ˈlɪvɪŋ ˈstændəd/ B1 20 diverse đa dạng adj /daɪˈvɜːs/ B2 21 slum khu ổ chuột n /slʌm/ B2 ghetto 22 overcrowding tình trạng quá tải dân số n /ˌəʊvəˈkraʊdɪŋ/ B2 23 shortage sự thiếu hụt n /ˈʃɔːtɪdʒ/ B1 lack, deficit abundance 24 survival sự sống sót n /səˈvaɪvl/ B1 death 25 frustration sự thất vọng, nản lòng n /frʌˈ...
                                                                                                        1. Question 1. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the aspects making the three cities known? A. modern architecture B. multiculturalism C. developed economies D. major coastal areas
                                                                                                        2. Question 2. The word “combine” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. A. integrate B. construct C. replace D. decide
                                                                                                        3. Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Urbanisation in Australia happens mostly in inland areas. B. Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane are small cities with little international influence. C. Australian cities aim to balance modern development with nature. D. Green spaces are often destroyed to make room for tall buildings.
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#2 | q=4 | nums=4, 5, 6, 7 | p=4-4 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. A city often whispers of opportunities: the chance to study in universities that shape the future, or to walk into hospitals where the latest technology can save lives. Therefore, people have always been drawn to cities, not only because they are larger and brighter, but also because they seem to promise something more. For many families, these possibilities feel like doors opening to a better tomorrow. Work, too, pulls people into the urban rhythm. In cities, offices light up late into the night, factories hum with energy, and cafés are full of quiet conversations about new projects. In cities, jobs are not just about survival; they offer a path to ambition. The chance to earn more, to dream bigger, becomes part of everyday life. And beyond work and study, cities hold a special charm in their culture. A theatre performance, a street festival, a late-night stroll through neon markets—these moments make life richer. People from different places meet, share stories, and create communities that could never exist in isolation. In this way, cities become more than concrete and glass; they become living spaces where energy, diversity, and creativity thrive.
                                                                                                        1. Question 4. The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to _______. A. cities B. people C. lives D. universities [Page 4]
                                                                                                        2. Question 5. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? “In cities, jobs are not just about survival; they offer a path to ambition.” A. City jobs mainly help people cover their daily living expenses. B. Work in cities provides opportunities for people to achieve higher goals. C. Employment in cities ensures survival witho...
                                                                                                        3. Question 6. The word “isolation” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. A. loneliness B. limitation C. separation D. contact
                                                                                                        4. Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. People move to cities only because they are larger and brighter. B. Cities provide opportunities for education, work, and cultural experiences. C. Jobs in cities are focused only on survival, not ambition. D. Urban life prevents people from building diverse communities.
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#3 | q=8 | nums=8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | p=5-5 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. Urbanisation is the shift of people from rural areas into cities, and with that movement comes both hope and challenge. Cities rise higher, roads stretch farther, and lights burn brighter, but behind these changes lie questions about how people will live together in limited space. For many, urbanisation has been a symbol of progress. In Australia, for example, cities such as Melbourne and Sydney have become centres of opportunity where education, healthcare, and employment are within reach. Families who once lived far from schools or hospitals now find themselves surrounded by services that can transform their lives. In this sense, cities are magnets, drawing people with the promise of higher living standards and more diverse experiences. Yet urbanisation also reveals its darker side. The same growth that fills skylines with glass towers can also create slums at their base. Dhaka, one of the most densely populated cities in the world, struggles daily with overcrowding, pollution, and shortages of housing. Traffic jams steal hours from workers, and rising living costs make survival difficult for the poor. Without balance, the dream of the city easily turns into frustration. That is why planning becomes so important. If governments invest in affordable housing, efficient transport, and green spaces, cities can grow without suffocating their people. Sustainable design, like Singapore’s vertical gardens or Copenhagen’s cycling culture, shows that urbanisation does not need to destroy quality of life. Instead, with thoughtful planning, it can create places where ambition and well- being walk hand in hand.
                                                                                                        1. Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “Urbanisation is the shift of people from rural areas into cities, and with that movement comes both hope and challenge.” A. Urbanisation refers to people being forced to abandon city life and return permanently to the rural areas they once left behind. B. Urbanisatio...
                                                                                                        2. Question 9. According to paragraph 2, living in cities means all of the following are within reach, EXCEPT _______. A. education B. entertainment C. healthcare D. employment
                                                                                                        3. Question 10. The word “their” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. A. families B. schools C. hospitals D. services
                                                                                                        4. Question 11. The word “reveals” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. A. alters B. releases C. hides D. shows
                                                                                                        5. Question 12. The word “suffocating” in paragraph 4 is CLOSEST in meaning to ______. A. refines B. hampers C. stifles D. fosters
                                                                                                        6. Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Urbanisation guarantees a perfect life for everyone who moves to the city. B. Cities like Dhaka prove that rapid growth always results in comfort and prosperity. C. Services such as education or healthcare become unavailable in modern cities. D. Sustainable planning shows that cities ca...
                                                                                                        7. Question 14. In which paragraph does the author suggest some solutions? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4
                                                                                                        8. Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention a waste of time due to traffic? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4

                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 profile hồ sơ cá nhân n /ˈprəʊfaɪl/ A2 2 upload tải lên v /ˌʌpˈləʊd/ B1 download 3 tag gắn thẻ v /tæɡ/ B2 untag 4 forum diễn đàn n /ˈfɔːrəm/ B2 5 comment bình luận v /ˈkɒment/ B1 remark 6 anonymous ẩn danh adj /əˈnɒnɪməs/ C1 unnamed, unidentified known, identified 7 misleading sai lệch, gây hiểu lầm adj /ˌmɪsˈliːdɪŋ/ C1 deceptive 8 newsfeed bảng tin n /ˈnjuːzfiːd/ B1 9 viewer người xem n /ˈvjuːə(r)/ B1 10 share chia sẻ v /ʃeə(r)/ B1 11 spread lan truyền v /spred/ B1 propagate 12 source nguồn n /sɔːs/ A2 13 reliable đáng tin cậy adj /rɪˈlaɪəbl/ B1 trustworthy, dependable unreliable, untrustworthy 14 exaggerated phóng đại adj /ɪɡˈzædʒəreɪtɪd/ C1 15 verify xác minh v /ˈverɪfaɪ/ C1 confirm, authenticate 16 flashy hào nhoáng adj /ˈflæʃi/ B2 gimcrack [Page 2] 17 credibility sự tin cậy, uy tín n /ˌkredəˈbɪləti/ C1 trustworthiness, reliability unreliability 18 advertisement quảng cáo n /ədˈvɜːtɪsmənt/ B1 commercial 19 access truy cập v /ˈækses/ B1 20 available có sẵn adj /əˈveɪləbl/ A2 unavailable 21 convincing thuyết phục adj /kənˈvɪnsɪŋ/ B2 persuasive, compelling unconvincing 22 fact-check kiểm chứng thông tin v /ˈfækt tʃek/ C1 23 news tin tức n /njuːz/ A1 24 the mass media phương tiện truyền thông đại chúng n.phr /ðə mæs ˈmiːdiə/ C1 25 magazine tạp chí n /ˌmæɡəˈziːn/ A1 26 interactive tương tác adj ...

                                                                                                        2. Question 2. A. upload B. fact-check C. tag D. spread
                                                                                                        3. Question 3. A. access B. comment C. share D. tag

                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương GLOSSARY No. Word/Phrase Pronunciation Meaning 1 anxious (adj) /ˈæŋk.ʃəs/ lo lắng 2 calm (v) /kɑːm/ làm giảm nhẹ, làm bình tĩnh 3 worry (v/n) /ˈwʌr.i/ lo lắng/ sự lo lắng 4 appearance (n) /əˈpɪə.rəns/ ngoại hình, diện mạo 5 relaxed (adj) /rɪˈlækst/ thư giãn 6 mind (n) /maɪnd/ tâm trí 7 harmless (adj) /ˈhɑːm.ləs/ vô hại 8 restless (adj) /ˈrest.ləs/ không ngủ được, bồn chồn 9 confuse (v) /kənˈfjuːz/ làm xáo trộn 10 remedy (n) /ˈrem.ə.di/ phương thuốc, cách chữa trị 11 symptom (n) /ˈsɪmp.təm/ triệu chứng 12 cure (v/n) /kjʊər/ chữa khỏi/ cách chữa 13 effective (adj) /ɪˈfek.tɪv/ hiệu quả 14 fat (n) /fæt/ chất béo 15 digestion (n) /daɪˈdʒes.tʃən/ sự tiêu hóa 16 consumption (n) /kənˈsʌmp.ʃən/ sự tiêu thụ 17 unhealthy (adj) /ʌnˈhel.θi/ không lành mạnh 18 exercise (v/n) /ˈek.sə.saɪz/ tập thể dục/ bài tập 19 muscle (n) /ˈmʌs.əl/ cơ bắp 20 mood (n) /muːd/ tâm trạng 21 workout (n) /ˈwɜː.kaʊt/ buổi tập luyện 22 mental (adj) /ˈmen.təl/ thuộc về tinh thần 23 body (n) /ˈbɒd.i/ cơ thể 24 avoid (v) /əˈvɔɪd/ tránh 25 take care of (phr) /teɪk keə əv/ chăm sóc 26 diet (n) /ˈdaɪ.ət/ chế độ ăn uống 27 ingredient (n) /ɪnˈɡriː.di.ənt/ thành phần 28 nutrient (n) /ˈnjuː.tri.ənt/ chất dinh dưỡng 29 recover (v) /rɪˈkʌv.ər/ hồi phục 30 life expectancy (n) /laɪf ɪkˈspek.tən.si/ tuổi thọ [Page 2] PRACTICE Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 30. 1. You might feel (1) _______ before an exam – your hands sweat, your stomach feels tight, and your mind jumps from tho...

Simply putting phones away earlier might be a quiet but powerful step toward better sleep.
                                                                                                        1. Question 7. A. relaxed B. effective C. mental D. harmless

                                                                                                        2. Question 19. A. worries B. symptoms C. workouts D. muscles

                                                                                                        7. Physical and (22) _______ health are like best friends—they go everywhere together. Skipping meals or (24) _______ rest can make both your body and your brain tired. So, (25) _______ care of your health isn't just about muscles—it's about mood, too!
                                                                                                        1. Question 22. A. anxious B. harmless C. mental D. unhealthy
                                                                                                        2. Question 23. A. body B. remedy C. worry D. expectancy

                                                                                                        4. Question 25. A. avoiding B. taking C. curing D. calming

                                                                                                        8. A balanced (26) _______ is important for staying healthy. It includes a variety of (27) _______ such as fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean meat. These foods provide essential (28) _______ like vitamins and minerals. A good diet gives us enough energy for daily activities such as studying, walking, or playing sports. It also helps the body stay strong and (29) _______ from illness. In the long run, eating well can improve life (30) _______ by lowering the risk of serious diseases. Therefore, choosing the right food is an important step toward a longer and healthier life.
                                                                                                        1. Question 26. A. digestion B. fat C. body D. diet
                                                                                                        2. Question 27. A. ingredients B. exercises C. muscles D. workouts
                                                                                                        3. Question 28. A. symptoms B. remedies C. nutrients D. worries
                                                                                                        4. Question 29. A. confuse B. recover C. exercise D. worry
                                                                                                        5. Question 30. A. digestion B. consumption C. appearance D. expectancy

                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#1 | q=3 | nums=1, 2, 3 | p=1-2 | rule_high
                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương GLOSSARY No. Word/ Phrase Pronunciation Meaning 1 lose weight (v) /luːz weɪt/ giảm cân 2 misunderstanding (n) /ˌmɪsʌndəˈstændɪŋ/ sự hiểu lầm 3 support (v) /səˈpɔːt/ ủng hộ, hỗ trợ 4 replace (v) /rɪˈpleɪs/ thay thế 5 combine (v) /kəmˈbaɪn/ kết hợp 6 whole grain (n) /həʊl ɡreɪn/ ngũ cốc nguyên hạt 7 sugary (adj) /ˈʃʊɡəri/ có đường, ngọt 8 control (v) /kənˈtrəʊl/ kiểm soát 9 overall (adj/adv) /ˌəʊvərˈɔːl/ tổng thể 10 immune system (n) /ɪˈmjuːn ˌsɪstəm/ hệ miễn dịch 11 deprivation (n) /ˌdeprɪˈveɪʃn/ sự thiếu hụt, mất mát 12 concentrate (v) /ˈkɒnsntreɪt/ tập trung 13 anxiety (n) /æŋˈzaɪəti/ sự lo lắng 14 depression (n) /dɪˈpreʃn/ trầm cảm 15 angry (adj) /ˈæŋɡri/ tức giận 16 upset (adj) /ʌpˈset/ buồn, khó chịu 17 check-up (n) /ˈtʃek ʌp/ cuộc kiểm tra sức khỏe 18 disease (n) /dɪˈziːz/ bệnh 19 protect (v) /prəˈtekt/ bảo vệ 20 improve (v) /ɪmˈpruːv/ cải thiện BẢNG CẤU TRÚC STT Cấu trúc Nghĩa 1 help somebody do something giúp ai đó làm việc gì 2 make a decision đưa ra quyết định 3 lead to something dẫn đến điều gì đó 4 take care of chăm sóc 5 talk to somebody nói chuyện với ai đó [Page 2] PRACTICE Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 1 to 3. Many people believe that exercise alone can help them lose weight, but that is a common misunderstanding. Exercise supports weight loss, but it cannot replace a healthy diet. Physical activities like walking, running, or swimming help burn calories, build muscles, and keep the body strong. However, without eating properly, exercise won't ...
                                                                                                        1. Question 1. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the benefits of physical activities? A. burn calories B. replace a healthy diet C. build muscles D. keep the body strong
                                                                                                        2. Question 2. The word “supports” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. A. prevent B. assist C. respect D. improve
                                                                                                        3. Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Exercise is more important than eating healthy for losing weight. B. People can lose weight by exercising alone without changing their diet. C. A combination of regular exercise and a balanced diet is best for weight loss. D. Eating fast food in small amounts is encouraged for a healthy ...
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#2 | q=4 | nums=4, 5, 6, 7 | p=3-3 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. Lack of sleep is a serious problem that affects both physical and mental health. When people don’t get enough rest, their bodies cannot recover properly. This can lead to headaches, tiredness, and a weakened immune system, making it easier to get sick. In addition to physical health, sleep deprivation also harms mental performance. It becomes harder to concentrate, remember things, and make good decisions. Students who do not sleep enough may find it difficult to study and perform poorly on exams. Over time, it can even lead to stress, anxiety, or depression. Lack of sleep causes many troubles in daily life. People who don’t sleep enough often feel tired and have no energy to do their tasks. They may forget important things or make mistakes at work or school. Sleepy people also get angry or upset more easily, which can lead to problems in relationships. In serious cases, lack of sleep can cause accidents, especially when driving or using machines. That is why getting enough rest every night is very important.
                                                                                                        1. Question 4. The word “weakened” in paragraph 1 is opposite in meaning to _______. A. famous B. important C. crowded D. enhanced
                                                                                                        2. Question 5. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the mental problems caused by a lack of sleep? A. stress B. depression C. tiredness D. anxiety [Page 3]
                                                                                                        3. Question 6. The word “They” in paragraph 3 refers to _______. A. Many troubles B. People C. Tasks D. Important things
                                                                                                        4. Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Sleep deprivation improves students’ performance on exams. B. People who don’t sleep enough may have problems in their relationships. C. Lack of sleep makes the immune system stronger. D. Sleepy people are more likely to stay focused and make better decisions.
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#3 | q=8 | nums=8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | p=4-4 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. Being healthy does not only mean having a strong body but also having a clear and positive mind. Both physical and mental health are important, and they work together to help us live well, study better, and enjoy life. Good physical health means your body is working properly. To stay physically healthy, people should eat a balanced diet, drink enough water, get regular exercise, and sleep well. It is also important to have regular health check-ups. These habits help the body grow stronger, prevent diseases, and give us energy for daily activities. Mental health is about our thoughts, feelings, and how we handle stress. Good mental health means feeling calm, confident, and in control. People can protect their mental health by talking to others, relaxing, avoiding too much stress, and doing things they enjoy. Sometimes, even small things like listening to music or walking in nature can improve your mood. In conclusion, both physical and mental health are important. If you are physically strong but feel sad or stressed all the time, you cannot enjoy life. On the other hand, if your mind is happy but your body is weak or sick, you may not have the energy to do anything. So, we must take care of both to live a full and healthy life.
                                                                                                        1. Question 8. The word “regular” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. A. huge B. hard C. easy D. rare
                                                                                                        2. Question 9. The word “they” in paragraph 3 refers to ______. A. people B. others C. things D. feelings
                                                                                                        3. Question 10. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the ways to protect mental health? A. relaxing B. talking to others C. avoiding too much stress D. working when you like
                                                                                                        4. Question 11. The word “handle” in paragraph 3 can be best replaced by _______. A. lose B. address C. consider D. join
                                                                                                        5. Question 12. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 4? “Good physical health means your body is working properly.” A. A healthy mind can keep the body from getting tired. B. Having a healthy body means you are always happy and confident. C. Physical health depends only on how strong your muscles are. D. Being physically ...
                                                                                                        6. Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. People with weak bodies cannot enjoy life even if they feel happy. B. Regular check-ups are not necessary for good physical or mental health. C. Physical health has no effect on how we study or enjoy daily life. D. Being mentally healthy means people never feel sad or overwhelmed again.
                                                                                                        7. Question 14. In which paragraph does the author describe what life is like without either physical or mental health? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4
                                                                                                        8. Question 15. In which paragraph does the author explain mental health? A. Paragraph 1 B. Paragraph 2 C. Paragraph 3 D. Paragraph 4

                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương GLOSSARY No. Word Pronunciation Meaning 1 growing (adj) /ˈɡrəʊ.ɪŋ/ đang gia tăng 2 negative (adj) /ˈneɡ.ə.tɪv/ tiêu cực 3 mental health (n. phr) /ˈmen.təl helθ/ sức khỏe tinh thần 4 natural disaster (n. phr) /ˈnætʃ.ər.əl dɪˈzɑː.stər/ thảm họa thiên nhiên 5 life-threatening (adj) /ˈlaɪfˌθret.ən.ɪŋ/ đe dọa tính mạng 6 shelter (n) /ˈʃel.tər/ nơi trú ẩn 7 damage (n/v) /ˈdæm.ɪdʒ/ thiệt hại / làm hư hại 8 hunger (n) /ˈhʌŋ.ɡər/ nạn đói 9 nutrition (n) /njuːˈtrɪʃ.ən/ dinh dưỡng 10 racism (n) /ˈreɪ.sɪ.zəm/ phân biệt chủng tộc 11 treat (v) /triːt/ đối xử 12 discrimination (n) /dɪˌskrɪm.ɪˈneɪ.ʃən/ sự phân biệt đối xử 13 division (n) /dɪˈvɪʒ.ən/ sự chia rẽ 14 peer pressure (n. phr) /ˈpɪər ˌpreʃ.ər/ áp lực từ bạn bè đồng trang lứa 15 push (v) /pʊʃ/ thúc ép 16 resist (v) /rɪˈzɪst/ chống lại 17 accept (v) /əkˈsept/ chấp nhận 18 bully (v/n) /ˈbʊl.i/ bắt nạt / kẻ bắt nạt 19 body shaming (n. phr) /ˈbɒd.i ˌʃeɪ.mɪŋ/ chê bai ngoại hình 20 offensive (adj) /əˈfen.sɪv/ gây xúc phạm 21 awareness (n) /əˈweə.nəs/ nhận thức 22 face (v) /feɪs/ đối mặt 23 cyberbullying (n) /ˈsaɪ.bəˌbʊl.i.ɪŋ/ bắt nạt qua mạng 24 victim (n) /ˈvɪk.tɪm/ nạn nhân 25 affect (v) /əˈfekt/ ảnh hưởng 26 challenge (n) /ˈtʃæl.ɪndʒ/ thách thức 27 unemployment (n) /ˌʌn.ɪmˈplɔɪ.mənt/ sự thất nghiệp 28 crime rate (n. phr) /kraɪm reɪt/ tỷ lệ tội phạm 29 affordable (adj) /əˈfɔː.də.bəl/ có thể chi trả được 30 infrastructure (n) /ˈɪn.frəˌstrʌk.tʃər/ cơ sở hạ tầng [Page 2] PRACTICE Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered b...

                                                                                                        2. A natural (4) _______, like an earthquake or flood, can destroy everything in seconds. It leaves people homeless, injured, or even dead. These events are often (5) _______, especially when there’s no warning. For example, the 2010 earthquake in Haiti killed over 200,000 people and left millions without (6) _______, clean water, or medical care.
                                                                                                        1. Question 4. A. disaster B. infrastructure C. challenge D. division
                                                                                                        2. Question 5. A. growing B. offensive C. life-threatening D. negative
                                                                                                        3. Question 6. A. shelter B. awareness C. unemployment D. hunger

The longer the war lasts, the harder it is for communities to survive and rebuild.
                                                                                                        1. Question 7. A. pressure B. damage C. challenge D. discrimination
                                                                                                        2. Question 8. A. Hunger B. Discrimination C. Cyberbullying D. Unemployment

Over time, this unfair treatment can create deep (13) _______ in society and increase tension between different communities.

                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương GLOSSARY No. Word Pronunciation Meaning 1 norm (n) /nɔːm/ chuẩn mực, quy tắc xã hội 2 taboo (n/ adj) /təˈbuː/ điều cấm kỵ; cấm kỵ 3 disrespectful (adj) /ˌdɪs.rɪˈspek.t.fəl/ thiếu tôn trọng 4 celebration (n) /ˌsel.əˈbreɪ.ʃən/ lễ kỷ niệm 5 ritual (n) /ˈrɪtʃ.u.əl/ nghi thức 6 religious (adj) /rɪˈlɪdʒ.əs/ thuộc về tôn giáo 7 connected (adj) /kəˈnek.tɪd/ có sự kết nối 8 multicultural (adj) /ˌmʌl.tiˈkʌl.tʃər.əl/ đa văn hóa 9 tradition (n) /trəˈdɪʃ.ən/ truyền thống 10 culture shock (n. phr) /ˈkʌl.tʃər ʃɒk/ sốc văn hóa 11 language barrier (n. phr) /ˈlæŋ.ɡwɪdʒ ˈbær.i.ər/ rào cản ngôn ngữ 12 overcome (v) /ˌəʊ.vəˈkʌm/ vượt qua 13 cultural identity (n. phr) /ˈkʌl.tʃər.əl aɪˈden.tə.ti/ bản sắc văn hóa 14 reflect (v) /rɪˈflekt/ phản ánh 15 value (n/v) /ˈvæl.juː/ giá trị; coi trọng, đề cao 16 ancestor (n) /ˈæn.ses.tər/ tổ tiên 17 rooted (adj) /ˈruː.tɪd/ bén rễ, ăn sâu 18 vary (v) /ˈveə.ri/ thay đổi, dao động 19 accepted (adj) /əkˈseptɪd/ được chấp nhận 20 impolite (adj) /ˌɪm.pəlˈaɪt/ bất lịch sự 21 expect (v) /ɪkˈspekt/ kỳ vọng, trông đợi 22 celebrate (v) /ˈsel.ə.breɪt/ ăn mừng, tổ chức 23 gather (v) /ˈɡæð.ər/ tụ họp 24 speciality (n) /ˌspeʃ.iˈæl.ə.ti/ đặc sản 25 costume (n) /ˈkɒs.tʃuːm/ trang phục 26 encounter (v) /ɪnˈkaʊn.tər/ gặp phải, đối mặt 27 embrace (v) /ɪmˈbreɪs/ đón nhận 28 international (adj) /ˌɪn.təˈnæʃ.ən.əl/ quốc tế 29 reject (v) /rɪˈdʒekt/ từ chối 30 misunderstanding (n) /ˌmɪs.ʌn.dəˈstæn.dɪŋ/ sự hiểu lầm [Page 2] PRACTICE Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the num...

These interesting differences reflect how customs shape behaviours worldwide.
                                                                                                        1. Question 18. A. vary B. overcome C. reject D. gather
                                                                                                        2. Question 19. A. accepted B. international C. impolite D. cultural

For example, when a student in a(n) (28) _______ exchange program respected local customs, she was warmly welcomed and formed lasting relationships. In contrast, some fail to accept or even (29) _______ cultural uniqueness. They may experience more disagreement and (30) _______, leading to prejudice and conflict.
                                                                                                        1. Question 26. A. expect B. vary C. encounter D. value

                                                                                                        [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 coastal thuộc vùng ven biển adj /ˈkəʊstl/ B2 2 unsustainable không bền vững adj /ˌʌnsəˈsteɪnəbl/ C2 sustainable 3 decline suy giảm v /dɪˈklaɪn/ B2 decrease, diminish increase, rise 4 critical nghiêm trọng, then chốt, quan trọng adj /ˈkrɪtɪkl/ B2 5 livelihood kế sinh nhai n /ˈlaɪvlihʊd/ B2 6 alternative lựa chọn thay thế / thay thế n/adj /ɔːlˈtɜːnətɪv/ B2 7 effect tác động n /ɪˈfekt/ B1 impact, influence 8 recovery sự phục hồi n /rɪˈkʌvəri/ B2 9 regulation quy định n /ˌreɡjuˈleɪʃn/ B2 rule 10 cooperation sự hợp tác n /kəʊˌɒpəˈreɪʃn/ B2 collaboration 11 species loài n /ˈspiːʃiːz/ B2 12 extinction sự tuyệt chủng n /ɪkˈstɪŋkʃn/ C1 13 destabilise làm mất ổn định v /ˌdiːˈsteɪbəlaɪz/ stabilise 14 permanently vĩnh viễn adv /ˈpɜːmənəntli/ B2 temporarily 15 cut down chặt, đốn hạ; cắt giảm phr. v /kʌt daʊn/ B1 reduce increase 16 packaging bao bì n /ˈpækɪdʒɪŋ/ B2 [Page 2] 17 lifespan tuổi thọ n /ˈlaɪfspæn/ C2 18 consumption sự tiêu dùng n /kənˈsʌmpʃn/ C1 use 19 natural resource tài nguyên thiên nhiên n. phr /ˈnætʃrəl rɪˈzɔːs/ B2 20 lessen làm giảm v /ˈlesn/ C1 reduce, diminish increase 21 container vật chứa, hộp đựng n /kənˈteɪnə(r)/ B2 22 extend kéo dài v /ɪkˈstend/ B2 prolong, lengthen 23 furniture đồ nội thất n /ˈfɜːnɪtʃə(r)/ A2 24 repurpose tái sử dụng cho mục đích khác v /ˌriːˈpɜːpəs/ C1 25 emission khí thải n /ɪˈmɪʃn/ C1 26 recycle tái chế v /ˌri...

                                                                                                        3. Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Japan's fish population has remained stable due to advanced fishing methods. B. Aquaculture has replaced traditional fishing across all of Japan. C. The Japanese government has done nothing to stop overfishing. D. Poor fishing practices have led to a sharp decline in bluefin tuna populat...
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#2 | q=4 | nums=4, 5, 6, 7 | p=5-5 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. The first “R” in the 3Rs stands for Reduce, which means cutting down on the amount of waste we produce. This can be done by avoiding unnecessary packaging, choosing products with a longer lifespan, and limiting single-use items. Reducing consumption at the source not only saves natural resources but also lessens the burden on landfills and the environment. The second “R” is Reuse, which encourages people to use items multiple times before throwing them away. Reusable shopping bags, containers, and glass bottles are examples of how we can extend the life of everyday products. Even old clothes and furniture can be repurposed or donated. Reusing helps reduce demand for new goods and lowers production-related emissions. The third “R” refers to Recycle, which involves turning used materials into new products. Paper, plastic, metal, and glass can all be collected and recycled into reusable materials. Recycling conserves resources and reduces energy usage compared to making products from raw materials. However, it should be the last option after reducing and reusing.
                                                                                                        1. Question 4. The word “lessens” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. A. values B. minimises C. declines D. increases
                                                                                                        2. Question 5. The word “them” in paragraph 2 refers to _______. A. people B. items C. shopping bags D. multiple times [Page 5]
                                                                                                        3. Question 6. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? “Reusing helps reduce demand for new goods and lowers production-related emissions.” A. Reusing stops factories from making new products and reduces energy use. B. Using items more than once cuts demand and lowers emissions from production. C. Buying second-hand goods...
                                                                                                        4. Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? A. Recycling is more important than reducing and reusing in waste management. B. Reuse involves turning used materials into something entirely new again. C. Reducing waste helps save resources and ease pressure on the environment. D. The 3Rs promote buying more single-use products to help t...
                                                                                                        vocab_context_group seg#3 | q=8 | nums=8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | p=6-6 | rule_high
                                                                                                        Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. While many people focus on emissions from transport and industry, agricultural practices also contribute significantly to climate change. Farming, especially large-scale animal agriculture, plays a major role in global warming and the increase of greenhouse gases. It’s essential to know about this connection to make good decisions about food consumption. One key issue is that livestock farming produces large amounts of methane. It is a powerful greenhouse gas released through animal digestion and manure. In addition, deforestation for grazing land reduces the planet’s ability to absorb carbon dioxide. Fertilisers used in crop farming also emit nitrous oxide, another potent greenhouse gas. Together, these factors make agriculture a major contributor to rising global temperatures. To address the problem, some countries are promoting sustainable farming techniques. These methods include improving animal diets to reduce methane, using organic fertilisers, and rotating crops or growing trees alongside crops. New technologies are also being tested, such as special feed that helps animals release less methane and growing food in tall indoor buildings, known as vertical farms. Ultimately, individual choices matter. Reducing meat consumption – especially beef – can lower demand for resource-intensive livestock farming. Instead, choosing fish, seafood, or plant-based meals helps reduce one’s carbon footprint and supports a more climate-friendly food system.
                                                                                                        1. Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? “It’s essential to know about this connection to make good decisions about food consumption.” A. Making food choices without knowledge can lead to poor long-term decisions. B. Understanding nutrition is vital to eating the right amount of food each day. C. Knowing the...
                                                                                                        2. Question 9. The word “produces” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to_______. A. captures B. digests C. consumes D. generates
                                                                                                        3. Question 10. The word “It” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. A. Digestion B. Key issue C. Methane D. Livestock farming
                                                                                                        4. Question 11. The word “release” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. A. capture B. create C. discharge D. refuse [Page 6]
                                                                                                        5. Question 12. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the sustainable farming techniques? A. using organic fertilisers B. rotating crops C. improving animal diets D. growing trees instead of crops
